text: The future of AI brings both exciting possibilities and serious questions. While current systems are designed for specific tasks, researchers are working toward "general AI" or AGI—machines that could think and learn across many areas like a human. Potential benefits include breakthroughs in medicine, education, and solving global problems. However, there are also risks, such as job displacement, privacy concerns, and ethical dilemmas about control. Most experts predict AGI may arrive within the next 20 to 50 years, though some think it will take longer. Because of these uncertainties, many believe we must carefully guide its development now to ensure it helps humanity rather than harms it.
